title: POLLN-RTT Round 5: Master Schema
description: Master schema combining all schemas for Conditional Geometry, Foldable Tensors, Channel Depth, and Folding Group. This unified schema provides a comprehensive framework for the POLLN-RTT Round 5 project.

geometricSpace.description: X: Geometric space with coordinates and manifold structure

probabilityDistribution.description: P: Probability distribution over the space

transformation.description: Ψ: Transformation mapping (X, P, C) → (X', P', C')

montyHallFold.description: Monty Hall Fold operation: T_fold(p)_i = p_i + p_opened

FoldableTensorFramework.description: Complete Foldable Tensor Framework F = (F_flat, C, P, K)

creasePattern.description: C: Crease pattern (topology of fold boundaries)

ChannelDepthFramework.description: Channel Depth and Cognitive Cost Framework

conditionalGeometryToFoldableTensor.description: Mapping between geometric space and tensor representation

coordinateEmbedding.description: How coordinates map to tensor indices

foldableTensorToFoldingGroup.description: Mapping between tensor folds and group elements

creaseToPermutation.description: How crease patterns relate to permutations

foldToZ2Tuple.description: How fold choices map to Z₂ tuples

visitAccumulation.description: How visits are tracked across channels

typescriptInterfaces.description: Master TypeScript interfaces combining all schemas

conditionalGeometryToFoldableTensor.coordinateEmbedding: coordinates → tensor indices via linear indexing

foldableTensorToFoldingGroup.creaseToPermutation: each crease induces a permutation on indices
foldableTensorToFoldingGroup.assemblyVerification: hash chain verified by group action

channelDepthToCognitiveCost.visitAccumulation: tensor operations tracked as channel visits

metadata.author: Schema Design Agent
metadata.description: Master schema for Conditional Geometry and Foldable Tensors

validationRules.description: All component schemas must be referenced
All component schemas should have consistent version
Integration layer should define mappings between all frameworks

summary.name: Conditional Geometry Transformation
summary.description: Simultaneous transformation of space, probability, and conditions

summary.name: Folding Group
summary.description: Semidirect product of flip choices and permutations
